response.justification: While Bogdan demonstrates excellent software craftsmanship, testing practices, and the ability to manage complex systems, he lacks the core technical requirements for this role. His expertise centers on web/backend services with TypeScript and Node.js, and he has minimal practical experience with Java, no Kotlin experience, and no demonstrated knowledge of the Spring ecosystem or IDE tooling. For a mid-senior IC position requiring immediate contribution to enterprise IDE development, the ramp-up time needed to acquire these specialized skills makes this an unsuitable fit.
strengths: Demonstrated mastery of software craftsmanship with strong focus on TDD, clean architecture, and open-source contributions (GISCollective, Fluent-Asserts, Trial)
Proven ability to design and maintain complex distributed systems at scale (micro-services at Dunnhumby, Direct Mail platform handling millions of transactions)
Excellent communication and knowledge-sharing skills evidenced by workshops, conference talks, and community leadership
concerns: Minimal practical Java experience (listed in skills but only briefly mentioned at Altom and National Instruments) with no deep JVM expertise demonstrated
Zero experience with Spring ecosystem or other enterprise Java frameworks (Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate) which are core to the role
No background in IDE development, language tooling, or IDE internals—career focused on web apps, mobile, and backend services rather than developer tool infrastructure
